summary: Created test_soundness_regression.py with 22 passing tests (+1 xfailed) across 5 test classes: boundary vacuity documentation (G(G(p)) and F(G(p)) both return None at M=2), shift closure verification, TaskFrame axiom validation on serialized oracle output, 100+ sequential call state isolation with mixed temporal depths, and 5 known-boundary-unsafe formula suite with documented mechanisms.
key_findings: F(G(p)) returns None at M=2 (spurious theorem, same as G(G(p))): both exhibit inner-G boundary vacuity
All 3 TaskFrame axioms (nullity, converse, forward_comp) hold in oracle's serialized task_relation output
Shift closure at M=2 is trivially satisfied (no valid non-zero shifts in domain {-1,0,1})
100+ sequential calls with depth-0/1/2 formulas show no state leakage
M=3 shift closure test xfailed as predicted (skolem_abundance over-constraint documented)
